Identity.History: Kumaragupta I ruled the Gupta Empire around 415–455 AD, inheriting a vast and prosperous realm from Chandragupta II. His reign was largely stable, marked by continued wealth, administration, coinage, and patronage of Brahmanical religion, especially the war god Skanda, from whom he took the title Mahendraditya. He likely maintained Gupta authority across northern India, though late in his reign the empire faced growing pressure from the Pushyamitras and possibly early Hun-related threats. He was succeeded by Skandagupta, who defended the empire during a more turbulent age.
